Duane Morris 1 role · 2 matches · best 160
Business Reorganization and Financial Restructuring Associate (PA/NY/TX/SF)View Job ↗
2026-03-17
New York City, New York; San Francisco, California; Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; Dallas, Texas · Bankruptcy · 3–5 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Duane Morris LLP seeks an associate to join its Business Reorganization and Financial Restructuring practice group. The ideal candidate should have 3-5 or more years of sophisticated commercial bankruptcy practice including both litigation and transactional experience. Candidate must have an aptitude for client contact, have the willingness to work independently, be a team player, be able to work/thrive in a fast-paced environment, and be detail-oriented. Stellar academics and superior writing and research skills are required. This position is available in our New York, Philadelphia, Dallas or San Francisco office along with requisite bar admission in the jurisdiction of practice. The target annual pay range for this role is $250,000-$295,000. Actual pay will vary depending upon various factors, including relevant experience, skill-set, current business needs and market factors. The compensation range listed is just one component of Duane Morris’ total comp package for employees, which, depending on the position, may also include discretionary bonuses and incentive packages, and firm-sponsored benefit programs.

Asset Management AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-17
District of Columbia; New York City, New York · Corporate · 2–7 yrs · 7 elite matches
Show job description
We are looking for associates with private equity and hedge fund experience between Classes of 2024 and 2019 to join our Asset Management team in DC or NY. The Firm pays market salaries to our attorneys. As such, below is the current associate base salary scale: * 1st year: $225,000 * 2nd year: $235,000 * 3rd year: $260,000 * 4th year: $310,000 * 5th year: $365,000 * 6th year: $390,000 * 7th year: $420,000 * 8th year: $435,000 The actual salary offered will be based on a number of factors including but not limited to the qualifications of the applicant, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and if applicable, the location in which the applicant lives and/or from which they will be performing the job. Salary Range $310,000—$435,000 USD

Greenberg Traurig 1 role · 5 matches · best 192
Capital Markets Associate (Mid to Senior Level)View Job ↗
2026-03-17
New York City, New York · Corporate · 5–7 yrs · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a Mid-Level Associate in the Capital Markets Group in New York. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Capital Markets Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Job Requirements: Ideal candidates will have 5 - 7 years of experience, from a peer BigLaw firm, in public and private equity and debt offerings, including initial public offerings and follow-on offerings, proxy statements and S-4 registration statements in connection with business combination transactions, interfacing with the SEC and other regulators, and familiarity with the ongoing reporting requirements under the ’34 Act for public companies, including 10-Ks, 10-Qs, 8-Ks and proxy statements. Experience in representing both issuers and underwriters would be beneficial. Candidates also should have day-to-day experience counseling public companies, including with respect to corporate governance matters. Candidates should possess a strong academic background, client management skills and drafting and negotiation skills. Candidates must have the skills to retain and grow existing clients and relationships. Candidates also should be admitted to the New York bar. The expected pay range for this position is: $365,000-$420,000. Salary will be determin

Corporate M&A and Securities Senior AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-17
Atlanta, Georgia; Austin, Texas; Boston, Massachusetts; Charlotte, North Carolina; Chicago, Illinois; Dallas, Texas; Denver, Colorado; Ft. Lauderdale, Florida; Houston, Texas; Jacksonville, Florida; Tampa, Florida; Los Angeles, California; Miami, Florida; New York City, New York; Orlando, Florida; Portland, Oregon; San Francisco, California; Stamford, Connecticut; Tysons Corner, Virginia; District of Columbia; Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; Orange County, California; Nashville, Tennessee; Seattle, Washington · Corporate · 5–7 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Job Description: Holland & Knight LLP seeks a senior-level associate to join its dynamic Corporate M&A and Securities Practice. Ideal candidates will have 5-7 years of experience with an emphasis on M&A and private equity transactions. Candidates must have a strong working knowledge of acquisition agreements and the full suite of documentation used in M&A and private equity transactions, as well as proficiency in drafting and negotiating the same. A proven track record supporting private equity sponsors and portfolio companies across the investment lifecycle (initial platform acquisitions, add-ons, portfolio company governance and exits), and familiarity with middle-market deal structures, are strongly preferred. Candidate must have strong interpersonal and communication skills, a high degree of maturity and business sense, and the ability to effectively manage internal and external client relationships. Excellent academic credentials are required and large national or international law firm experience is preferred. Holland & Knight possesses a collegial atmosphere and provides its professionals with a large international platform with tangible opportunities for professional growth and advancement. Job Requirements: 5 to 7 years of experience JD from an ABA Accredited Law School Must be licensed or ready to become licensed to practice law in the Virginia. Norton Rose 2 roles · 2 matches · best 164
Projects Associate (M&A/Dev)View Job ↗
2026-03-17
Austin, Texas; Houston, Texas; Dallas, Texas; Chicago, Illinois; Denver, Colorado; Los Angeles, California; San Francisco, California; New York City, New York; St. Louis, Missouri; District of Columbia · Energy · 3–6 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Norton Rose Fulbright is seeking an Associate to join its Projects team. The ideal candidate will have 3 to 6 years of relevant experience and a strong background in domestic and international business transactions and corporate matters, particularly in the energy sector, with location flexibility across any of our US offices. Candidates should have a strong focus on domestic and international business transactions and corporate matters, particularly in the energy sector. This role offers the opportunity to work on high-profile projects involving renewable energy, energy storage, and power generation technologies. Collaborating across our global team, we act on some of the most innovative, complex and challenging projects in the world. Lawyers would have the opportunity to work on 100% renewable energy and energy transition transactions, if desired, or a broader energy and infrastructure practice. This role involves handling a range of M&A transactions, including acquisitions, joint ventures, divestitures of projects, portfolios of projects, and entire development platforms. The role may also include project development work if desired. Our projects practice is one of the largest in the world, with over 350 project and project finance lawyers located across our 50+ offices. We represent lenders, sponsors, developers, institutional investors, contractors, operators, and government entities in major domestic and cross-border renewable energy projects and financings. Key Requirements: tailor to specific job 5+ years’ of experience as a projects finance lawyer. Experience drafting and negotiating the principal financing documentation. Experience with renewable energy projects preferred. Experience reviewing project documentation preferred. Strong academic credentials. Location: This position may be based in New York, Chicago, D.C., Los Angeles, and Texas offices. Applicants should indicate their office(s) of interest in their application. The full time base annual salary for this position in the New York City and Los Angeles market is expected to range between $365,000- $435,000. This range represents the firm’s good faith and reasonable estimate of the range of possible compensation at the time of posting for a position in New York City and Los Angeles. Salaries in other markets will vary depending on market data. Bonus opportunities are non-guaranteed and are dependent upon individual and firm performance. Position Information: This is open to Senior Associate and Senior Counsel level candidates. The Senior Counsel position offers a compelling opportunity to build a high‑impact career within a prestigious global law firm, working on sophisticated matters alongside top‑tier partners and clients. Senior Counsel is a partnership‑track role, designed for attorneys seeking long‑term career investment, meaningful responsibility, and significant advancement potential. Mergers & Acquisitions Associate Attorney - MidlevelView Job ↗
2026-03-17
Phoenix, Arizona; Denver, Colorado; Chicago, Illinois; New York City, New York; Dallas, Texas; Seattle, Washington · Corporate · 3–6 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Job Description: One of Fortune’s Best Places to Work, Perkins Coie, LLP, a leading international law firm, is seeking Mergers & Acquisitions associates for our Seattle, Denver, Chicago, Dallas, Phoenix and New York offices. We’ve created a firm culture that is based on collaboration, dedication to serving our clients and mutual respect. We are seeking experienced associates to join our expanding Mergers & Acquisitions group. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials and a minimum of three years of experience handling a variety of M&A transactions, including private equity and/or other private target transactions. Experience at an AmLaw 100 firm is required. If you are interested in joining the team, please access the link below to provide your cover letter, resume, law school transcript and deal sheet. This position is eligible for an annual discretionary bonus, 401(k) plan, medical, dental, and vision insurance, personal medical and parental leave, up to ten paid Holidays, and family care benefits. More information regarding benefits and programs may be found here Compensation range: $260,000 to $390,000 annually. Compensation depends on qualifications and experience. #LI-Hybrid

Reed Smith 1 role · 1 match · best 166
San Francisco - Labor & Employment Associate (Mid-to-Senior-Level)View Job ↗
2026-03-17
San Francisco, California · Labor & Employment · 5–? yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
The Labor & Employment practice group is looking to hire an experienced mid-senior level associate to join our San Francisco office. The candidate must have five or more years of experience in employment litigation, including single plaintiff cases and wage and hour class actions. Candidate must also be familiar with Federal Court Rules and E-Discovery. This is a terrific opportunity for an attorney who is interested in being groomed towards partnership to help lead a diverse, supportive team within a friendly office community, has the drive to help build and grow the practice and seeks to work with fun clients on interesting cases. The successful candidate must have excellent writing analytical skills, experience handling multiple cases, and be able to serve as point person on litigation, including supervising junior associates and staff and serving as client contact. Candidate must have demonstrated project management skills and be able to take a case and run with it. Deposition and/or courtroom experience required; employment advice and counseling experience desired. Candidate should possess strong academic credentials from a nationally recognized law school and be licensed to practice in California. Reed Smith values collegiality and diversity among our lawyers and seeks to make the practice both professionally and personally rewarding. We offer a competitive salary and benefits. Troutman Pepper 2 roles · 2 matches · best 169
Transactional Real Estate Associate - AtlantaView Job ↗
2026-03-17
Atlanta, Georgia · Real Estate · 2–5 yrs · 1 elite match
Show job description
Troutman Pepper Locke’s Real Estate practice group is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Associate to join our dynamic team in the Atlanta office. The ideal candidate will have 2 to 5 years of substantive experience in real estate transactional matters, with expertise in real estate debt or equity matters. The successful candidate will handle a variety of real estate transactions, including acquisitions, dispositions, leasing, and development projects, and provide legal advice and support on real estate financing, including debt and equity structuring. Responsibilities include drafting, reviewing, and negotiating complex real estate documents such as purchase agreements, leases, and financing documents, collaborating with clients to understand their business objectives and providing strategic legal guidance, conducting due diligence, and managing closing processes for real estate transactions. The salary range for this position is $235,000 - $310,000, dependent on relevant years of experience. Candidates must be licensed to practice law and in good standing in Georgia. For more information about the practice visit our website here. For inquiries or additional information on this position, please contact Tonja Washington . Troutman Pepper Locke’s Real Estate practice group is seeking a mid-level associate to be based in the Charlotte, Richmond or Atlanta office with 4 to 6 years of real estate transactional experience, including acquisitions, dispositions, and leasing. The ideal candidate will also have experience with real estate debt or equity matters. They will be confident and comfortable working with clients daily, will have an elevated attention to detail and excellent communication skills. Responsibilities include drafting, reviewing, and negotiating complex real estate documents such as purchase agreements, leases, and financing documents, collaborating with clients to understand their business objectives and providing strategic legal guidance, conducting due diligence, and managing closing processes for real estate transactions. The salary range for this position is $295,000 - $350,000, dependent on relevant years of experience. Candidates must be licensed and in good standing in the state in which they practice. For more information about the practice visit our website here. For inquiries or additional information on this position, please contact Tonja Washington. Winston & Strawn 4 roles · 17 matches · best 193
New York Office - Energy and Infrastructure Transactions AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-17
New York City, New York · Energy · 3–6 yrs · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn’s New York office is looking for a mid-level associate who has experience in the energy (particularly power generation and transmission) and infrastructure sectors to be part of highly regarded team. The work focuses on acquisitions and dispositions of and equity investments in projects and portfolios, development of projects, and financings. Experience should include performing and supervising due diligence, drafting of principal and ancillary agreements, coordinating with specialists (tax, environmental, real estate, regulatory, etc.) and managing closings. All candidates should have excellent academic credentials from an accredited law school, as well as strong organizational, analytical, writing, oral communication, and people skills. Candidate should be someone who thrives in a collaborative environment working closely with other lawyers (both partners and associates) and clients. All candidates must be admitted to practice in New York. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

New York Office – Structured Finance AssociateView Job ↗
2026-02-04
New York City, New York · Banking · 4–? yrs · 6 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn is looking for a mid-level associate to join its structured finance group. Ideal candidates will have at least four years of structured finance experience. We are looking for candidates who have strong deal experience at a law firm with a focus in structured finance, lending, securitization, or similar finance structures. Experience across a variety of asset types and structures is preferred but not required. We are looking for candidates to be placed in our New York office. All candidates must be admitted to the New York State Bar. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

Multi Office - M&A-Mid-Level AssociateView Job ↗
2026-02-11
Charlotte, North Carolina; Chicago, Illinois; Dallas, Texas; Houston, Texas; Los Angeles, California; New York City, New York; San Francisco, California; Silicon Valley, California; District of Columbia; Miami, Florida · Corporate · 4–6 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Winston & Strawn is looking for a mid-level associate to join their corporate practice group. The ideal candidate will have between four and six years of M&A and private equity experience, specifically in representing financial sponsors and strategic buyers/sellers in connection with all varieties of M&A and private equity transactions, including the preparation of acquisition/disposition agreements and related matters. While Chicago is our preferred location for this position, candidates for our other U.S. offices will be considered. All candidates must be admitted to the state bar of their preferred location. We are an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds and experiences. View our associate salary disclosures.

2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Banking · 2020–2022 · 1 elite match
Show job description
The Finance group in New York is seeking a candidate with four to six years of Finance experience (i.e., class of 2022-2020). Expertise in various types of secured lending transactions and familiarity with drafting and negotiating security interest opinions would be ideal. Candidate would be expected to develop an expertise in advising clients on UCC issues, with an emphasis on Article 9 Secured Transactions and Article 8 Investment Securities matters. The work primarily consists of reviewing security documentation, advising clients on personal property security interest issues and structures and reviewing security interest opinions. Candidate would also be expected to keep abreast of any upcoming changes to the UCC and related laws and to help with the Firm’s knowledge management materials in this field. Candidate would support Finance attorneys as well as attorneys in various other transactional and non-transactional practice areas. High level of academic achievement and solid law fi

San Francisco, California Job Description Position Summary: Ideal candidates will be problem solvers, self-motivated, and have extensive first-hand proficiency with counseling businesses on major global and U.S. data protection legal frameworks, the behind-the-scenes workings of the Internet, AI systems, and privacy and regulatory compliance, in addition to superior writing and communication skills, comfort with ambiguity, and a proven record of providing exemplary client service. This is a partner track position. Ideal applicants will have 5 – 6 years of experience focusing on regulatory investigations or litigation involving consumer protection issues (State AG, FTC, and other regulatory agencies), and/or be well-versed in defending data privacy and security class actions (e.g., Stored Communications Act, wiretapping claims, claims involving allegedly unfair and deceptive trade practices, and data breach class actions). Fund Finance Associate (3-6+ Years) - New York, NY OfficeView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Banking, Corporate · 2020–2023 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Cadwalader is seeking associates with 3-6 years of relevant experience for its Fund Finance practice. Qualified candidates will have experience in syndicated lending, commercial lending, leverage finance, fund formation, CLOs, asset-based lending, NAV financings, or acquisition financings. Candidates must possess excellent academic credentials and solid law firm legal experience. Selected candidates will get extensive interaction with preeminent bank, asset manager and lending clients. The anticipated annual base salary range for this position is $260,000 to $420,000. The actual base salary offered will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, the qualifications of the applicant, years of relevant experience, and, if applicable, the location from which the applicant will be performing the job.

As an AmLaw100 firm, this will offer a unique opportunity to a lateral associate who wants to be part of an expanding team. This opportunity is open to multiple large markets. You possess excellent academic credentials and strong writing, analytical, and communications skills and are a member of the bar in a state in which Nelson Mullins has an office. Hunton seeks an attorney with at least four years of project finance experience to join the Energy and Infrastructure team in a full-time partnership-track associate position in our New York office. Prior experience at a national or international law firm strongly preferred. Candidates must possess excellent oral and written communication skills, demonstrate initiative and potential for leadership, and have the judgment and confidence to work independently with clients and colleagues. New York bar membership is preferred, and in any event required within one year of joining the firm. EEO/drug-free workplace/E-Verify participant/Veteran/Disability. At the time of posting, the expected salary range for this position for full-time on-track associates is $310,000 to $435,000. Mergers & Acquisitions Resource AttorneyView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Corporate · ≤2022 · 27 elite matches
Show job description
Job Description: Cooley is a leading global law firm with offices across the US, Europe and Asia. Our clients, ranging from entrepreneurs to emerging companies to Fortune 50 corporations, partner with us on transformative deals, complex IP and regulatory matters and high-stakes litigation where innovation meets the law. As a firm we constantly strive to deliver the highest quality work and superior service on every matter entrusted to us. Our lawyers are organized into two departments—Business and Litigation—which are further organized into practice groups. Mergers & Acquisitions Resource AttorneyView Job ↗
2026-03-26
Silicon Valley, California · Corporate · ≤2022 · 13 elite matches
Show job description
Job Description: Cooley is a leading global law firm with offices across the US, Europe and Asia. Our clients, ranging from entrepreneurs to emerging companies to Fortune 50 corporations, partner with us on transformative deals, complex IP and regulatory matters and high-stakes litigation where innovation meets the law. As a firm we constantly strive to deliver the highest quality work and superior service on every matter entrusted to us. Our lawyers are organized into two departments—Business and Litigation—which are further organized into practice groups. Given our uncompromising commitment to the quality of work that we produce, remaining on, if not defining, the leading edge of the law in a business and legal environment that is evolving daily, and working efficiently and cost-effectively, we are intently focused on enabling our practitioners and practice groups to the greatest extent possible. Regulatory, Licensing and Enforcement AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
Houston, Texas; Seattle, Washington; Portland, Oregon; Sacramento, California; San Francisco, California; Silicon Valley, California; Los Angeles, California; Orange County, California; West Virginia; New York City, New York; District of Columbia; Boston, Massachusetts; Austin, Texas; Chicago, Illinois; Miami, Florida; Charlotte, North Carolina · Banking · 2022–2024 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Responsibilities Orrick is seeking a junior to mid-level associate with 2-4 years of experience to join our market-leading Fintech and Financial Advisory practice. This role involves assisting clients in the financial services industry with a variety of cutting-edge payments, regulatory, licensing, and government enforcement matters. The ideal candidate will have experience with, and be responsible for, supporting senior attorneys and clients with the following tasks: • Performing legal research on payments and consumer financial services regulatory matters; • Analyzing and communicating in writing and orally with clients and senior attorneys; regarding regulatory risk and business operations; • Conducting licensing reviews, including multi-state legal surveys; • Participating in licensing applications and licensing maintenance matters; • Participating in supervisory matters at the examination stage, including collection and production of information and developing responsive informati

Kanner & Pintaluga 1 role · 1 match · best 168
Plaintiff Bad Faith AttorneyView Job ↗
2026-03-26
Boca Raton, Florida · Litigation · ≤2021 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Founded in 2003, Kanner & Pintaluga is a NLJ500 and Mid-Market Pro 50 law firm that has recovered over $1 billion for property damage and personal injury clients nationwide. With nearly 100 lawyers and more than 30 offices throughout the Central and Southeastern United States, our primary goal is to achieve the most favorable outcome for our clients, who have the absolute right to receive the maximum compensation for their damages. POSITION SUMMARY: Kanner & Pintaluga, a dynamic and rapidly growing law firm, handles auto accidents, premise liability, medical malpractice, wrongful death cases, nursing home abuse and first-party home and business property damage throughout the State of Florida. We are seeking an experienced Plaintiff Bad Faith Attorney to join our Litigation team in Boca Raton, Florida. The ideal candidate will have significant experience in first-party property insurance and/or personal injury bad faith litigation, with the ability to identify, develop, and preserve

Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher 1 role · 3 matches · best 177
Labor & Employment (OC)View Job ↗
2026-03-26
Orange County, California · Labor & Employment · 2017–2021 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking an associate in law school class years 2017 through 2021 to join the labor and employment team. The practice is primarily employer-side litigation, with some advice and investigations work as well. Litigation is mostly class and representative actions, both wage and hour and discrimination, and experience in the class actions space would be helpful. Three years+ in a peer law firm in the employment space would likely have requisite experience. The annual compensation range for this position is between $310,000 and $435,000. The salary offered within this range will depend upon qualifications and other operational considerations. Benefits offered for this position include health care; retirement benefits; paid days off, including sick time, and vacation time; parental leave; basic life insurance; Flexible Spending Accounts; as well as discretionary, performance-based bonuses.

Tech Transactions Lateral Associate (Bay Area)View Job ↗
2026-03-26
San Francisco, California; Silicon Valley, California · Corporate · 2020–2022 · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Baker Botts L.L.P. is seeking a mid-level associate with 4–6 years of experience to join our Technology Transactions practice in the Palo Alto or San Francisco office. The ideal candidate will have significant experience advising clients on a broad range of technology-focused commercial transactions, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating agreements involving the development, licensing, commercialization, and acquisition of technology and intellectual property. This role is well-suited for candidates seeking a mix of standalone commercial work and deal-driven technology transactions in fast-paced, highly strategic environments. Responsibilities: • Drafting and negotiating complex technology and intellectual property agreements, including SaaS, cloud services, licensing, and development agreements • Advising on the technology and IP aspects of M&A, private equity, and venture capital transactions, including due diligence, representations and warranties, IP structuring, and

Washington, D.C. - Investment Funds, Regulatory AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
District of Columbia · Corporate · 2023–2025 · 4 elite matches
Show job description
Kirkland & Ellis LLP is seeking an associate with outstanding credentials and qualifications to join the market-leading regulatory practice of our Investment Funds Group. Our funds regulatory practice offers a dynamic experience ranging from assisting fund sponsors with fundraising and transactions to navigating SEC examination and enforcement matters. The ideal candidate will have 1-3 years of investment funds-related regulatory experience at a global law firm, including: reviewing offering materials and fund governing documents; advising clients on compliance with applicable rules and regulations; preparing SEC filings; and responding to examinations. Strong familiarity with the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 required; familiarity with the Investment Company Act of 1940 and Securities Act of 1933 preferred. Candidates must be admitted to practice in the District of Columbia. Kirkland & Ellis LLP offers a competitive salary structure as well as a robust offering of benefits to all o

We are looking for an associate to join our Strategic Transactions and Licensing group (the “Tech Group”) in Austin, Los Angeles, Silicon Valley, San Francisco, or Ann Arbor*. Our practice offers early responsibility, direct client contact, and exposure to a wide variety of technology, life sciences, AI, financial services, and consumer products companies at all stages of growth. This role is well-suited for associates who want to accelerate their development as technology and commercial counselors while working with a collaborative, high-performing team. As a Tech Group associate, you will: * Draft and negotiate licensing, partnering, and commercial agreements * Provide intellectual property, privacy, and product counseling directly to clients * Manage transactions across all stages of our clients’ businesses, from formation and financings to buy- and sell-side acquisitions and beyond.. ABOUT THE ROLE Clark Hill is seeking an attorney with at least three (3) years of Labor & Employment litigation experience to join its Labor & Employment team in the Los Angeles, CA office. This position focuses on single-plaintiff employment litigation and class action/PAGA cases. This is a full-time position with a hybrid work schedule. REQUIRED EXPERIENCE AND SKILL SET * Drafting pleadings, workplace policies, and discovery * Drafting and arguing motions, and conducting administrative hearings * Taking and defending depositions * Conducting employee trainings * Providing day-to-day advice and counseling to employers QUALIFICATIONS * Juris Doctor (JD) from an accredited law school * Must be currently licensed to practice law in the State of California or eligible for admission without examination. New York City, New York · Real Estate, Banking · ≤2022 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking a commercial real estate finance associate with 4 or more years of experience representing commercial and investment banks, insurance companies, debt funds and/or other regulated and non-regulated lenders in the financing of commercial properties. The ideal candidate should have experience conducting title, survey, lease and other commercial property diligence and working on the origination of loans secured by commercial properties. Exposure to mezzanine loans and commercial mortgage loan securitization and syndication is a plus. Candidates should expect a varied practice and anticipate THAT ALL OR A PORTION OF THEIR TIME MAY BE RELATED TO real estate owner/operator, developer and equity side work, INCLUDING LEASING. The anticipated annual base salary range for this position is $310,000 to $435,000. The actual base salary offered will depend on a variety of factors, including without limitation, the qualifications of the applicant and years of relevant experience.

We are seeking an associate with 6+ years of experience to join the National Security Group. Work with Spiegel & McDiarmid LLP partners on a wide range of legal matters including federal and state administrative law, public utility law, and climate and energy policy. Advocate for clients’ interests in a variety of settings, including state and federal administrative proceedings, utility rate cases, settlement negotiations, and meetings with agency officials, legislators, and counterparties. We are seeking associates in law school class years 2021 to 2024 to join our M&A and Private Equity teams. Associates will be expected to advise corporate clients and private equity funds in connection with acquisitions, dispositions, minority investments, other similar transactions and general corporate matters. Candidates must have significant experience handling M&A transactions, a good understanding of corporate law and market terms and substantial experience in drafting transaction documents and effective deal management. Driven personality, business development-oriented mindset, and ability to take on high level of responsibilities and work with autonomy are key. The role is expected to have significant direct client contact and will offer a great opportunity for entrepreneurial associates to develop relationships with clients and manage matters with a high degree of independence. District of Columbia · Government · 2023–2025 · 6 elite matches
Show job description
Skadden’s Political Law Compliance and Investigations Group is growing and seeking two junior associates (Class of 2025-2023) to join its Washington, DC office. This is a unique and exciting opportunity to join a top tier political law practice. Skadden’s Political Law Compliance and Investigations Group is strictly nonpartisan and does not represent political candidates or parties. Successful candidates must have a background in political law, exceptional legal research, writing and communications skills and a commitment to providing high quality service to clients. The position is best suited for candidates with a law firm background. The starting base salary for this Washington, DC position is expected to be between $225,000-$260,000 annually. Actual salary will be determined based on skills, experience (to the extent relevant) and other job-related factors, consistent with applicable law. This position may be eligible for a discretionary year-end bonus.

New York City, New York · Corporate · ≤2023 · 6 elite matches
Show job description
We are seeking attorneys for associate positions in Cahill Gordon & Reindel’s New York office for our Private Credit practice group. Candidates should have at least three years of experience in corporate work, with substantial exposure to private credit transactions including with direct lenders, private credit funds, and other institutional investors and investment banks in corporate finance transactions, leveraged buyouts, acquisition financings and recapitalization transactions. Preferred candidates will have experience in structuring and executing traditional first lien, unitranche, stretch senior, second lien and subordinated loan transactions, structured equity investments, including non-traditional preferred stock financings, and equity co-investments. Candidates must have excellent oral and written communication skills, a strong academic background and be admitted to the New York Bar. Show job description
Who We Are MoFo is a destination law firm for talented, ambitious attorneys who value collaboration, teamwork, and commitment to excellence. We are known around the world not only for delivering innovative yet practical client service but also for serving as an incubator for our top-notch talent. MoFo consistently receives top-tier rankings from leading directories and publications, speaking to our legal capabilities, dedication to creating an inclusive workplace, and commitment to our communities. Our Real Estate Practice Group MoFo has one of the leading real estate practices in the United States, Asia, and Europe. We provide innovative and practical legal advice to a broad range of real estate industry participants in all types of real estate transactions, including acquisition, development, financing, ownership, leasing, and operation. Learn more about the team and the work you can do at https://www.mofo.com/capabilities/real-estate. Who You Are You have law firm experience in acqu

NYC Mid-level/Senior Corporate AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Corporate · 2020–2021 · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Our Capital Markets & Corporate Department seeks an Associate with 5-6 years of corporate law firm experience, including some or all of the following: private equity, mergers & acquisitions, venture capital, joint ventures, general corporate and commercial agreements. This position offers the opportunity for significant responsibility and client contact. Successful candidates should possess excellent drafting and analytical skills, great attention to detail, high level of organization, strong verbal and interpersonal skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects and people, at times across offices. Superior academic credentials and admission to the New York Bar are required. Large or mid-size law firm experience preferred. We currently expect 3 days in-office, including Wednesdays. The salary range that we expect to pay for this position is a minimum of $285,000 and a maximum of $305,000 annually. The actual pay wage may vary based on experience or other relevant factors

Brown Rudnick Signed Client 1 role · 1 match · best 171
Bankruptcy & Corporate Restructuring AssociateView Job ↗
2026-03-26
New York City, New York · Bankruptcy · 2021–2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
Bankruptcy & Corporate Restructuring Associate Dispute Resolution & Restructuring Brown Rudnick seeks a talented 3rd to 5th year associate to join the Bankruptcy & Corporate Restructuring group in New York. The ideal candidate will have restructuring experience including Chapter 11 cases, representing debtors and creditors in all areas of corporate restructurings and bankruptcy proceedings. Candidates should be capable of handling and running multiple significant matters at once and should be able to work collaboratively within a team. Our Dispute Resolution & Restructuring Department represents a diverse range of clients across high-stakes, complex matters in a fast-paced and collaborative environment. The team includes high-performing, creative, and experienced lawyers with deep expertise in Chapter 11 reorganizations, out-of-court workouts, catastrophic corporate loss, mass tort, liquidations, fixed charge and administrative receiverships, restructured credits and securitization

Boston, Massachusetts · Litigation · ≤2023 · 1 elite match
Show job description
MG+M The Law Firm (MG+M) is seeking to add an experienced associate attorney in our Boston office to support our New England asbestos practice group. This is a hybrid role. MG+M is a nationally renowned litigation law firm concentrating on complex civil matters. The firm’s expertise is diverse but focused in the areas of toxic tort, complex product liability, local government, general liability, and commercial litigation. We approach litigation as a unified team, integrating our efforts with those of our clients to advance their interests wherever they do business. Our goal is always to deliver optimal results at the best possible value. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Handle local and national accounts throughout New England states. Independently manage a litigation caseload. Conduct depositions. Prepare witnesses for deposition and trial. Review and analyze discovery requests. Prepare and respond to discovery responses and pleadings. Prepare court filings and service to counse

JOB TITLE: ATTORNEY DEPARTMENT: MASS TORT REPORTS TO: BONNIE RICKERT FLSA STATUS: EXEMPT ROLE SUMMARY Hilliard Law’s mission for our clients is clear - the “Relentless Pursuit of Justice.” We strive to find talent that pursues excellence for our clients. We believe our success is a result of our people, and we’re looking for individuals who bring their experience, dedication, problem-solving skills, and creativity to further our mission. Hilliard Law is seeking a Mass Tort Attorney to join our dynamic legal team in a fast-paced, high-impact environment. This role is responsible for managing and litigating mass tort cases, advocating on behalf of clients who have been harmed by defective products, pharmaceuticals, or environmental hazards. The attorney will play a key role in case strategy, client communication, and all phases of litigation. Show job description
Dorsey & Whitney LLP is seeking a litigation associate with three to five years of experience to join our New York office. The ideal candidate will be interested in working on a variety of commercial disputes, along with some exposure to intellectual property and employment litigation matters. This associate will have the opportunity to work in all phases of litigation, from pleadings through discovery, dispositive motion practice, trial, and appeal. We anticipate that this position will involve responsibility over cases in federal and state court and in arbitration, and we expect that it will provide in-court experience and opportunities to work on motion and trial practice. Greenberg Traurig has an excellent opportunity for an M&A Associate to join the Corporate Practice of our Chicago office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of four years of significant experience in corporate transactions with an emphasis on public and private M&A, including private equity. Candidates must have a strong working knowledge of acquisition agreements and the other documentation used in complex M&A and private equity transactions and proficiency in drafting the same. This position requires a candidate with strong interpersonal skills, a high degree of maturity, and a proven willingness to accept significant responsibility and manage a challenging workload within a fast-paced environment. Strong academic credentials and writing skills are essential. Show job description
Our Commercial Litigation Practice specializes in representing clients in high-stakes business disputes across a wide range of industries. We provide expert legal services in areas such as contract disputes, class actions, business torts, shareholder and partnership disputes, antitrust litigation and intellectual property litigation. Our team handles all phases of litigation, from pre-litigation ccounseling and strategic planning to trial and appellate proceedings. By leveraging our deep legal expertise, global reach and strategic insights, we help clients navigate complex legal challenges, protect their business interests, and achieve favorable outcomes through both court proceedings and alternative dispute resolution methods. Roles at this level focus on handling legal tasks that require strong understanding of applicable laws related to Disputes matters. We provide expert legal services in areas such as contract disputes, class actions, business torts, shareholder and partnership disputes, antitrust litigation and intellectual property litigation. Our team handles all phases of litigation, from pre-litigation ccounseling and strategic planning to trial and appellate proceedings. By leveraging our deep legal expertise, global reach and strategic insights, we help clients navigate complex legal challenges, protect their business interests, and achieve favorable outcomes through both court proceedings and alternative dispute resolution methods. As an associate joining our practice, you will be given responsibility for a wide range of important tasks for our Firm’s clients. Responsibilities will vary, but generally include the following: Develop litigation strategy and draft substantive pleadings for ma

Show job description
Cadwalader’s Capital Markets Department is seeking an associate with 3 to 6 years of experience to join our Charlotte office. Candidates should have experience in drafting, reviewing, and negotiating transaction documents related to commercial mortgage loan securitizations, including pooling and servicing agreements, offering documents, mortgage loan purchase agreements, certificate purchase agreements, placement agency agreements, legal opinions and related contracts. Candidate should have experience managing the closing process for complex transactions. Candidates must demonstrate strong attention to detail, excellent communication skills, teamwork, a solid work ethic, and a proactive sense of ownership over their responsibilities. The anticipated annual base salary range for this position is $260,000 to $390,000. The New York City office of FBT Gibbons is seeking a Product, Tort & Insurance Litigation Associate. FBT Gibbons LLP is a national, full-service law firm formed through the combination of Frost Brown Todd and Gibbons, bringing together more than 800 attorneys across 25 offices. Our attorneys offer comprehensive legal solutions to clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies and global multinationals to startups and nonprofits. FBT Gibbons distinguishes itself as a dynamic, forward-moving firm with a coast-to-coast footprint and a reputation for excellence across multiple practice areas and industries. Our reputation is further reinforced by its numerous Chambers and Best Lawyers ranked practices and attorneys. We are a people‑first firm recognized for our award-winning inclusive culture and a strong commitment to community and pro bono work. Job Description At K&L Gates, we are looking for smart, imaginative and hard-working people with diverse backgrounds, experiences and ideas to join us. Perhaps our search for talented visionaries and your search for important and impactful work lead to the same place. We are seeking an experienced midlevel associate to join our Corporate M&A team. This role may be based in Boston, Charleston, Charlotte, Miami, Nashville, Pittsburgh, Portland, Raleigh, or Seattle. The ideal candidate will have three to five years of Corporate M&A experience, with private equity transaction experience preferred. Key qualifications include: • Strong M&A experience, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating merger agreements, stock purchase agreements, asset purchase agreements, letters of intent, and related documents. Show job description
The Woodland Hills office of Fisher Phillips seeks employment litigation associates with 3–6 years of experience. Ideal candidates will be comfortable drafting and responding to discovery and initial pleadings, drafting and arguing motions, as well as taking and defending depositions. Significant experience in labor and employment law and handling California wage-and-hour class action claims and PAGA cases is preferred, but candidates with strong litigation experience and a demonstrated interest in labor and employment law will be considered. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials, strong writing and analytical skills, and the ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment. Successful candidates will be self-starters with the ability to work independently and as part of a team. Candidates must be admitted in California and hold a J.D. from an ABA-accredited law school. Type of Experience: King & Spalding seeks a mid-level associate for its underwriting and structured finance and capital markets team in New York. Ideal candidates should have experience advising financial institutions on securities offerings under the Securities Act of 1933, including representing issuers and underwriters in registered and exempt transactions. Familiarity with disclosure requirements, offering documentation, and compliance matters under the 1933 Act is essential. Candidates with general capital markets or leveraged finance backgrounds and a strong interest in securities law are encouraged to apply. The practice provides for a mix of esoteric asset-backed securities offerings, registered equity underwriting offerings, public-company M&A, at-the-market offerings, and special purpose acquisition companies. Los Angeles, California · Litigation · 2019–2024 · 3 elite matches
Show job description
Firm Summary White & Case is an elite global law ﬁrm serving leading companies, ﬁnancial institutions and governments worldwide. Our long history as an international ﬁrm means we are perfectly placed to help our clients resolve their most complex legal challenges wherever they may be. With lawyers operating from more than 40 locations, working around the world, we have invested heavily in building a high-quality full-service practice competing at the top of the market. We are distinguished by our on-the-ground presence in the world’s key ﬁnancial markets and our strengths in handling complex cross-border work. It’s not just about our global network of ofices; it’s the global interconnectedness of the Firm that our people, and our clients, value most. We work well together across geographic and practice boundaries. It’s one of the reasons we attract and retain cross-border work. Our lawyers are globally minded, enterprising, collaborative and committed to excellence. Our people represen

Join our collaborative team of corporate lawyers working with exciting biotech and tech companies, guiding and supporting them from inception, through funding, and on to the IPO process and life as public company, or sale. We are looking for corporate attorneys with three to four years of experience for an associate or of counsel position with background and interest in the dynamic world of life sciences and tech. Desired expertise includes an ability to advise companies through the start-up and venture capital phase, IPOs and equity capital markets, and public company reporting. The position will be based in Palo Alto with a hybrid schedule. You will gain deep and broad experience working directly with clients bringing world-changing products and technologies to market. FBT Gibbons LLP is a national, full-service law firm formed through the combination of Frost Brown Todd and Gibbons, bringing together more than 800 attorneys across 25 offices. Our attorneys offer comprehensive legal solutions to clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies and global multinationals to startups and nonprofits. FBT Gibbons distinguishes itself as a dynamic, forward-moving firm with a coast-to-coast footprint and a reputation for excellence across multiple practice areas and industries. Our reputation is further reinforced by its numerous Chambers and Best Lawyers ranked practices and attorneys. Amundsen Davis seeks an experienced employment attorney to join our growing Management-Side Labor Employment team in either our Chicago, Crystal Lake, Rockford or St. Charles, IL okice. The ideal candidate holds an active Illinois bar license and has at least ﬁve years of substantial experience with providing comprehensive advice and ccounsel to employers, navigating proceedings before administrative agencies, and defense litigation in federal and state courts. Only those candidates with a background in employment litigation and providing employment advice and ccounsel will be considered. Experience with labor a plus but not required. Candidates must have strong analytical abilities, excellent communication skills and a deep commitment to client service. The annual base salary range anticipated for this position in Illinois is $160,000-$220,000, depending upon experience. This salary range excludes available bonus opportunities. Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, a full-service Fortune 100 ﬁrm, is seeking an attorney to work in its growing national management-side Employment, Labor & Workforce Management (ELWM) practice. Candidates should have a minimum of seven years of relevant labor and employment law experience but welcome all levels to apply. This position requires knowledge of federal and state labor & employment laws (e.g., wage and hour, Title VII of the Civil Rights Act, ADEA, FMLA, ADA, and similar state laws). Candidates must possess strong academic credentials and have excellent communication and writing skills. We have garnered national recognition for our demonstrated commitment to the recruitment, retention and advancement of qualiﬁed female and diverse attorneys. Admission to practice law in California is mandatory.
